behavior_summary: U8P_TROUGHEW::look is a simple U8P_TROUGHEW::look/examine handler that presents a one-word description 'trough' to the player.
After emitting the bark/text, the process reaches a suspend/terminate node — inferred to end or pause the U8P_TROUGHEW::look interaction immediately after displaying the description.
No global flags are read or written, and there are no calls to external systems or other game objects; the routine is purely presentational.
notable_control_flow: Single linear path: emit bark/text 'trough' -> suspend/terminate.
No branching, menus, or conditional logic are present.
No external calls or side-effects besides displaying text are observed.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' node always fully terminate the U8P_TROUGHEW::look process for this object, or can it be resumed by engine logic? (inferred 'ends' the handler; confidence: low)
Is the displayed text used anywhere else (e.g., inspection logs or UI tooltips) beyond the immediate bark? (likely just immediate display; confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_TROUGHEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines the TROUGHEW object; display the word 'trough' and then suspend/end the U8P_TROUGHEW::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player uses the 'U8P_TROUGHEW::look'/'examine' action on the TROUGHEW object (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_TROUGHEW::look
function_map.role: U8P_TROUGHEW::look/examine handler that displays a short descriptive bark ('trough')
